content.text:
Context: 5 launch themes — minimal-dark, minimal-light, terminal, glass, developer
Objective: Design and implement the 5 launch themes. These define the aesthetic identity of @kntic/links. Quality over quantity — each theme should feel complete and polished, not half-baked. All themes implement the full CSS custom property contract from LINKS-006.

Guidelines:
1. Read .kntic/MEMORY.MD for current project context and memory.
2. Reference the project ADRs in .kntic/adrs/ for architectural standards.
3. Execute the task in the manifest.
4. Update the manifest status to 'ready_for_merge' once the task is complete.
5. Adhere to security best practices (Least Privilege) for any infrastructure changes.
6. Update .kntic/MEMORY.MD if you learned something new or changed the architecture.

Use the kntic-task-status skill to set the task status. You MUST call .kntic/lib/skills/kntic-task-status/set-status.sh .kntic/manifests/LINKS-007.json ready_for_merge when done, or .kntic/lib/skills/kntic-task-status/set-status.sh .kntic/manifests/LINKS-007.json needs_review if blocked.

Please proceed with the task defined in: .kntic/manifests/LINKS-007.json

{
  "task_id": "LINKS-007",
  "title": "5 launch themes \u2014 minimal-dark, minimal-light, terminal, glass, developer",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "priority": "high",
  "description": "Design and implement the 5 launch themes. These define the aesthetic identity of @kntic/links. Quality over quantity \u2014 each theme should feel complete and polished, not half-baked. All themes implement the full CSS custom property contract from LINKS-006.",
  "requirements": {
    "features": [
      "Create src/themes/minimal-dark.css \u2014 DEFAULT theme. Clean, modern dark. Background ~#0f0f0f, white text, subtle zinc borders, soft rounded links. Accent: a muted blue or violet. No gradients, no gimmicks. This is what most users will ship.",
      "Create src/themes/minimal-light.css \u2014 Mirror of minimal-dark in light mode. Off-white background (#f9f9f9), near-black text, same clean aesthetic. Comfortable for daylight reading.",
      "Create src/themes/terminal.css \u2014 Classic terminal green-on-black. Background #0a0a0a, foreground #00ff41 (matrix green), monospace font (JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, Courier fallback). Link hover effect: green glow. ASCII-style border characters on links via CSS (or simple 1px solid green borders). Cursor blink animation on the name.",
      "Create src/themes/glass.css \u2014 Glassmorphism. Semi-transparent backdrop-filter blur cards on a dark gradient background (#0a0a1a \u2192 #1a0a2e). Links are frosted glass panels: rgba white background at low opacity + border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1). Soft purple/blue accent. Subtle glow on hover.",
      "Create src/themes/developer.css \u2014 Developer/hacker aesthetic. Dark background, monospace everywhere (system-ui fallback to Menlo/Monaco/Courier), links styled as code blocks or terminal commands. Accent: orange (#FF4500, KNTIC orange). Left border bar on links (like VSCode sidebar). Filename-style labels in the link buttons.",
      "Every theme file: full CSS reset at top, :root block with all required custom properties, complete styling for: body, .page-container, .profile-section, .avatar, .name, .bio, .links-list, .link-item, .link-item:hover, .link-icon, .link-label, .link-description, .footer. No external font imports (use system fonts or Google Fonts via @import only \u2014 no JS)",
      "Verify all 5 themes work with the theme loader (correct filename convention)"
    ],
    "outputs": [
      "src/themes/minimal-dark.css",
      "src/themes/minimal-light.css",
      "src/themes/terminal.css",
      "src/themes/glass.css",
      "src/themes/developer.css"
    ],
    "files_to_create": [
      "src/themes/minimal-dark.css",
      "src/themes/minimal-light.css",
      "src/themes/terminal.css",
      "src/themes/glass.css",
      "src/themes/developer.css"
    ]
  },
  "context_scope": {
    "read_only": [
      ".kntic/adrs/",
      "src/themes/README.md",
      "src/themes/loader.js",
      "src/generator.js"
    ],
    "write_access": [
      "src/themes/minimal-dark.css",
      "src/themes/minimal-light.css",
      "src/themes/terminal.css",
      "src/themes/glass.css",
      "src/themes/developer.css",
      ".kntic/manifests/LINKS-007.json"
    ]
  },
  "notes": "KNTIC orange is #FF4500 \u2014 use it sparingly as accent in the developer theme. Each theme should be self-contained (no theme imports another). Aim for roughly 80-150 lines of CSS per theme \u2014 enough to be polished, not enough to be bloated. Google Fonts @import is allowed for terminal/developer themes (JetBrains Mono).",
  "created_at": "2026-03-16T19:10:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-03-16T21:28:36.414915"
}

# MEMORY.MD — Persistent Working Memory

>
> **Last Updated:** 2026-03-16 (LINKS-006 completed)

---

## 1. Current Project State

**Project:** Links
**Repository:** *(update with your gitlab host/group/project)*

### Completed Tasks (Merged)
| Task ID | Title | Key Output |
|---------|-------|------------|
| LINKS-001 | Repo scaffold — package.json, CLI entry point, project structure | `package.json`, `src/cli.js`, 10 command stubs, `LICENSE`, `.npmignore`, `.gitignore`, `package-lock.json` |
| LINKS-002 | links.yaml config schema and parser | `src/config.js` — DEFAULT_CONFIG, validateUrl, validateConfig, findConfig, readConfig, writeConfig; atomic writes via tmp+rename; no external validation deps |
| LINKS-003 | `links init` — scaffold a new link page project | `src/commands/init.js` — creates links.yaml with commented template, --force flag, optional [directory] arg, uses os.userInfo() for default name |
| LINKS-004 | `links add / remove / list` — link management commands | `src/commands/add.js`, `src/commands/remove.js`, `src/commands/list.js` — add/remove/list links in links.yaml; URL validation, case-insensitive label matching, --update flag, --json output, scheduled link display |
| LINKS-005 | `links deploy --self` — static HTML generator | `src/generator.js` — generatePage(config, options), filterScheduled(), loadThemeCSS(), inlineImage(); `src/commands/deploy.js` — --self, --out, --open flags; `src/themes/minimal-dark.css` — default theme; fully self-contained HTML with inlined CSS, base64 avatar, schedule filtering, XSS-safe escaping |
| LINKS-006 | Theme system — CSS-based, pluggable | `src/themes/loader.js` — loadTheme(name), listThemes(); `src/themes/README.md` — documents 17 CSS custom property contract; `minimal-dark.css` updated to standard tokens; directory-traversal protection via path.basename |

### Active Services
- **Dashboard** → `http://localhost:8000`
- **Orchestrator** → `kntic-engine` container

---

## 2. Architectural Constraints

Refer to `.kntic/adrs/` for all platform-level constraints:

| ADR | File | Title | Key Rule |
|-----|------|-------|----------|
| ADR-001 | ADR-001-Bootstrapping.md | Manifest-Driven Orchestration | FSM statuses: todo, in_progress, refactoring, needs_review, ready_for_merge, merged, backlog |
| ADR-002 | ADR-002-Security-Isolation.md | Security Isolation — Least Privilege | Mount only what the service needs; default `:ro`; dashboard sandboxed |
| ADR-003 | ADR-003-Task-Schema.md | Task Manifest Schema | Required fields: task_id, title, status; omission rule (no null/empty) |
| ADR-004 | ADR-004-GIA-State-File-Contract.md | GIA State File Contract | `.kntic/gia/state.json` — atomic writes; pass/fail only; dashboard read-only |
| ADR-005 | ADR-005-Technical-Debt-Task-Convention.md | Technical Debt Task Convention | TD- prefix; mandatory MEMORY.MD §3 update on resolution |
| ADR-006 | ADR-006-ADR-Guidelines.md | ADR Guidelines | Mandatory sections: Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences |
| ADR-007 | ADR-007-Proposal-Format-And-Handling.md | Proposal Format and Handling | Proposals in `.kntic/docs/`; feeds into ADR pipeline |

---

## 3. Known Technical Debt

| ID | Description | Severity | Origin |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|
| TD-1 | Orchestrator (`kntic-engine`) mounts `.:/app` full project root — should be narrowed to specific paths for least-privilege compliance. | Medium | ADR-002 §1 |

---

## 4. Active Protocol Notes

### For Task Agents
1. **Read this file first** at the start of every session.
2. **Update this file last** after completing a task.
3. **Manifest lifecycle:** `in_progress` → `ready_for_merge`. Orchestrator sets `merged`.
4. **Scope discipline:** Only read/write files listed in your manifest's `context_scope`.
5. **Security first:** Default to `:ro` for volume mounts. Escalate only with justification (ADR-002 §6).
6. **Actions log:** Append one entry to the manifest's `actions` array per session (ADR-003).
7. **New manifests:** Apply `chmod 664` + `chown root:node` immediately after creating (ADR-002 §4).

### Volume Mount Strategy (ADR-002)
- Dashboard mounts are granular and `:ro` by default.
- Only `.kntic/manifests/` and `.kntic/docs/` are writable by the dashboard.
- The orchestrator (`kntic-engine`) currently mounts `.:/app` full access — narrowing this is tracked as TD-1 in ADR-002.
- Write access escalation requires manifest justification + ADR-002 §2 table update.

---

## 5. [Questions for Human]

_No open questions._

---

## 6. [Additional Info from Human]

_No additional info yet._

# KNTIC Links — Theme System

Themes are **plain CSS files** that live in `src/themes/`. No build step, no
preprocessor. Each file defines a complete visual identity for a link page by
implementing a fixed set of **CSS custom properties** (design tokens) on `:root`
and using them throughout.

---

## Quick start — creating a new theme

1. Copy an existing theme (e.g. `minimal-dark.css`) to a new file:

   ```bash
   cp src/themes/minimal-dark.css src/themes/my-theme.css
   ```

2. Edit the `:root` block to change colours, fonts, radii, etc.

3. Set the theme in your project's `links.yaml`:

   ```yaml
   theme: my-theme
   ```

4. Run `links deploy --self` to see the result.

---

## Custom property contract

Every theme **must** declare all of the following custom properties inside a
`:root { … }` rule. The generator and the base HTML structure depend on these
tokens — omitting any of them will produce broken or inconsistent output.

| Token | Purpose | Example value |
|---|---|---|
| `--bg-color` | Page background colour | `#0d0d0d` |
| `--bg-secondary` | Card / surface background | `#1a1a1a` |
| `--text-primary` | Primary text colour | `#f0f0f0` |
| `--text-secondary` | Secondary text colour (descriptions) | `#cccccc` |
| `--text-muted` | Muted / de-emphasised text | `#999999` |
| `--accent-color` | Primary accent (link text, highlights) | `#e0e0e0` |
| `--accent-hover` | Accent on hover / focus | `#ffffff` |
| `--link-bg` | Link card background | `var(--bg-secondary)` |
| `--link-bg-hover` | Link card background on hover | `#252525` |
| `--link-border` | Link card border colour | `#333333` |
| `--link-radius` | Link card border-radius | `8px` |
| `--link-padding` | Link card inner padding | `0.875rem 1.25rem` |
| `--font-body` | Body font stack | `-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif` |
| `--font-mono` | Monospace font stack (if needed) | `'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace` |
| `--avatar-radius` | Avatar image border-radius | `50%` |
| `--page-max-width` | Max width of the page content column | `480px` |
| `--footer-opacity` | Footer link resting opacity | `0.6` |

### Rules

* **No hardcoded colours outside `:root`** — every colour value used in
  selectors must reference one of the tokens above.
* Themes may add *extra* custom properties for internal use but must **not**
  remove or rename any of the standard tokens.
* The base reset (`box-sizing`, `margin`, `padding`) should be included in
  every theme so each theme is fully self-contained.

---

## Loader API (`src/themes/loader.js`)

The loader is a pure ES-module with two exports:

### `loadTheme(themeName)`

Resolves a theme name (e.g. `"minimal-dark"`) to the corresponding `.css` file
in the themes directory, reads it, and returns the raw CSS string.

* Throws a descriptive `Error` if the theme is not found (includes list of
  available themes).
* Sanitises the input to prevent directory-traversal (uses `path.basename`).

### `listThemes()`

Returns a sorted `string[]` of available theme names (filenames without the
`.css` extension).

```js
import { loadTheme, listThemes } from './themes/loader.js';

console.log(listThemes());       // ['minimal-dark']
const css = loadTheme('minimal-dark');
```

---

## File structure

```
src/themes/
├── loader.js          # Theme loader module
├── minimal-dark.css   # Default theme
├── README.md          # This file
└── <your-theme>.css   # Add your own here
```

---

## Integration with the generator

The generator (`src/generator.js`) loads the theme specified in `links.yaml`
and inlines the full CSS into a `<style>` block in the generated HTML. The
output is a single self-contained file — no external stylesheets.
